title: Ordinary Annuity vs Annuity Due
event: Reyes Co. will make five annual payments of $6,000 at an 8% rate (PV factor, ordinary annuity, 8%, 5 = 3.99271). Compute the present value if payments occur at the END of each year, and at the BEGINNING of each year.

computationPaths.narration: An annuity due starts one period sooner, so every payment is discounted one period less — multiply the ordinary factor by (1 + i).

memorize.body: Annuity-due factor = ordinary factor × (1 + i). Works for both PV and FV tables.
Read the timing words: 'beginning of each period,' 'payments start immediately,' 'first payment today' → annuity due.

questions.prompt: Five payments of $6,000, 8%, payments at the BEGINNING of each year. PV = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the ordinary annuity. Beginning-of-period payments are worth more — multiply by 1.08.
That's the undiscounted total (5 × 6,000). Present value discounts every payment.
